title: Xtra Buttery German Pork Bowl
description: A rich and savory German dish featuring tender pork, rose water, and a generous amount of butter, served in a hot dolsot stone bowl.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the pork tenderloin with paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
In a large skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium-high heat and sear the pork tenderloin until browned on all sides.
Transfer the pork to a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 15 minutes, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the pork is cooking, prepare the rose water butter by mixing the rose water with the remaining 4 tablespoons of butter in a small bowl.
Remove the pork from the oven and brush the rose water butter all over the pork tenderloin.
Wrap the pork in foil and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing it thinly.
To assemble the dish, place a scoop of cooked brown rice in the bottom of a hot dolsot stone bowl, followed by a few slices of the pork and a sprinkle of chopped parsley.
